assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as an admission webhook failure after control plane upgrade and explains that the cert-manager webhook is incompatible with Kubernetes 1.32

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' command to identify the problematic webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to Ignore and also offers nuclear option to delete webhook configurations entirely

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' commands

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'This allows pods to create but disables certificate validation' and emphasizes it's a nuclear option and temporary fix

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check current cert-manager version and explicitly mentions checking compatibility with K8s 1.32, stating 'you need cert-manager v1.15.0+'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides detailed upgrade instructions via Helm and manifests, specifying v1.16.0 as compatible with K8s 1.32 (note: expectation mentions 1.30 but context is 1.32 upgrade)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Presents failurePolicy: Ignore as the first immediate mitigation option before the more drastic deletion option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Includes multiple test commands: 'kubectl run test-pod' after webhook bypass and 'kubectl run test-pod-2' after full fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: While the response shows Helm upgrade commands, it does not explicitly mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like GitOps manifests or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Clearly labels it as 'Immediate Mitigation' and 'temporary', includes section to 'Restore Full Webhook Protection' with command to revert failurePolicy back to Fail
